rxn_class_summary: Transition-metal-free oxidative decarboxylative radical difluoroarylmethylation/cyclization of N-methacryloyl-2-arylbenzimidazoles with α,α-difluoroarylacetic acids to give difluoroarylmethyl-substituted benzimidazo[2,1-a]isoquinolin-6(5H)-ones

key_intermediates.name: α,α-difluorobenzyl radical (A) from decarboxylation of α,α-difluorophenylacetic acid by persulfate
tertiary C-radical B from addition of A to the methacryloyl alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical C from intramolecular ipso/ortho cyclization onto the 2-aryl ring
Wheland-type arenium cation D after SET oxidation of C
sulfate radical anion SO4•- (from thermolysis of S2O8 2-)

electron_flow: Persulfate thermally homolyzes to SO4•-, which oxidatively decarboxylates the α,α-difluoroarylacetic acid to a difluorobenzyl radical A; A adds across the methacrylamide alkene to give tertiary radical B, which undergoes intramolecular homolytic aromatic substitution onto the 2-aryl benzimidazole to form cyclohexadienyl radical C; SET oxidation of C by another SO4•-/S2O8 2- gives arenium cation D, and rearomatization by loss of H+ delivers the product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3aa not formed; TEMPO–CF2Ar adduct 4 detected by LC-MS, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; desired product 3aa not observed

probe_reagents.name: Phenylacetic acid (2r) in place of α,α-difluorophenylacetic acid
probe_reagents.observation: Decarboxylative coupling still proceeds to give 3ar in 68% yield, indicating the CF2 group has little influence on the transformation

notes: Mechanism is proposed by analogy to prior persulfate-mediated decarboxylative radical cyclizations; no DFT, KIE, EPR, or CV evidence is reported. X-ray crystallography of product 3ea confirms regiochemistry but does not directly probe the mechanism.
